_comment: Deterministic Google Ads optimization ruleset. Each rule is evaluated against real account signals (no AI). 'scope' is 'account' or 'campaign'. 'operator' is one of: is_true, is_false, gt, gte, lt, lte. Every rule cites an official Google source and a last-reviewed date.

rules.title: Conversion tracking is not set up
rules.why: Without conversion tracking, Smart Bidding cannot optimize and you cannot measure which clicks become sales or leads.
rules.fix: Create conversion actions (purchase, lead, call) and verify the tag fires, then allow 1–2 weeks of data before judging performance.

source.label: Google Ads Help — About conversion tracking

rules.title: Optimization score is below 85%
rules.why: Google's Optimization Score estimates how well your account is set to perform. A lower score means there are pending, validated improvements.
rules.fix: Open the Recommendations page and apply or dismiss each item; Google shows the score uplift for every recommendation.

source.label: Google Ads Help — About optimization score

rules.title: Campaign is limited by budget
rules.why: Search budget lost impression share above 10% means ads stop showing before the day ends, so you miss eligible traffic.
rules.fix: Raise the daily budget on profitable campaigns, or improve efficiency so each dollar buys more clicks.

source.label: Google Ads Help — About impression share

rules.title: Campaign is losing impressions to Ad Rank
rules.why: Search rank lost impression share above 20% means ads are held back by low Ad Rank (bid and/or Quality Score), not budget.
rules.fix: Improve Quality Score (ad relevance, expected CTR, landing page) and review bids or your bid strategy targets.

source.label: Google Ads Help — About Ad Rank

rules.title: Click-through rate is below the account benchmark
rules.why: A CTR below comparable inventory usually signals weak ad relevance or missing assets, which also lowers Quality Score.
rules.fix: Mirror the keyword in headlines, add more headlines/descriptions, and add sitelink and callout assets.

source.label: Google Ads Help — About Quality Score

rules.title: Some ad groups have only one ad
rules.why: With a single ad there is nothing to test, so click-through and conversion rates tend to stagnate.
rules.fix: Run at least two responsive search ads per ad group and keep ad rotation set to Optimize.

source.label: Google Ads Help — Tips for creating effective ads

rules.title: Responsive search ads have "Poor" Ad Strength
rules.why: Ad Strength measures relevance and diversity of your ad assets; "Poor" ads reach fewer auctions and perform worse.
rules.fix: Add the suggested headlines and descriptions until Ad Strength reaches "Good" or "Excellent".

source.label: Google Ads Help — About Ad Strength

rules.title: Some ads are disapproved
rules.why: Disapproved ads do not serve, so affected ad groups may show no ads at all and lose all their traffic.
rules.fix: Open the Ads page, review the policy reason on each disapproved ad, fix the violation, and resubmit for review.

source.label: Google Ads Help — Understand the status of your ads

rules.title: Campaign is spending with zero conversions
rules.why: Cost accrued over the last 30 days with no recorded conversions usually points to wasted spend, weak targeting, or missing conversion tracking.
rules.fix: Confirm conversion tracking is firing, add negative keywords from the Search Terms report, and tighten targeting; pause the worst offenders if they keep converting at zero.

source.label: Google Ads Help — Tips to improve performance

rules.title: Low search impression share
rules.why: Search impression share under 50% means your ads appear in fewer than half of the auctions they were eligible for, capping reach.
rules.fix: Address whichever is higher — budget lost or rank lost impression share — by raising budget or improving Quality Score and bids.

source.label: Google Ads Help — About impression share
